problem_statement: GET /flights/search returns an empty list when filtering by origin airport code provided in lowercase (e.g. origin=ath), even though matching flights exist and airport codes are stored in uppercase.
goal: Make flight origin search case-insensitive so lowercase or mixed-case origin codes match stored uppercase airport codes.
acceptance_criteria: Searching flights with origin=ath returns flights whose origin airport code is ATH.
Searching with uppercase, lowercase, or mixed-case origin values yields the same results.
Existing search behavior for other filters remains unchanged.
constraints: Issue explicitly points to backend/app/services/flight_service.py search_flights as the affected area.
Airport codes are stored in uppercase, so normalization should happen at comparison or query construction time.
unknowns: Whether the search uses exact equality, SQL LIKE, or an ORM filter in search_flights.
Whether other filters in search_flights should also be normalized for case-insensitive matching.
repo_summary: Backend application with flight service, API routes, schemas, models, tests, and seed scripts. The issue specifically targets the backend flight search service.

targeted_symbols.reason: Likely contains the origin filter logic that compares the request query param against stored airport codes; this is the smallest relevant implementation area named in the issue.
Best place to add or adjust a regression test proving lowercase origin codes match uppercase stored codes.

localization_rationale: The bug is directly attributed to search_flights in backend/app/services/flight_service.py.
The symptom is specific to origin airport code casing, suggesting a normalization or case-insensitive comparison bug in the service-layer filter rather than routing, schema, or frontend.
The minimal fix likely involves uppercasing the incoming origin parameter before filtering or applying a case-insensitive query operator.
A service test is the most relevant nearby validation point to confirm the behavior and prevent regressions.
